completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/__init__.py which imports and re-exports DispatchAttempt, SyncResult, AsyncPending, Degraded, DispatchError, DispatchOutcome, and CorrelationKey. The package's __all__ lists all seven names. Verified by tests/forge/dispatch/test_dispatch_models.py::TestPackageReExports::test_package_init_reexports_all_public_models.

All five Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes are defined in src/forge/dispatch/models.py with the field schema from the task brief: DispatchAttempt(resolution_id, correlation_key, matched_agent_id, attempt_no=1, retry_of=None); SyncResult(kind='sync_result', resolution_id, attempt_no, coach_score=None, criterion_breakdown=dict, detection_findings=list); AsyncPending(kind='async_pending', resolution_id, attempt_no, run_identifier); Degraded(kind='degraded', resolution_id, attempt_no, reason); DispatchError(kind='error', resolution_id, attempt_no, error_explanation). Mutable defaults use default_factory; each variant uses ConfigDict(extra='forbid'). Covered by TestDispatchAttempt and TestSyncResultDefaults.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `DispatchOutcome` exported as a discriminated union over the four variants.
completion_promises.evidence: DispatchOutcome = Annotated[Union[SyncResult, AsyncPending, Degraded, DispatchError], Field(discriminator='kind')] is defined in src/forge/dispatch/models.py and re-exported from forge.dispatch. Each variant carries a Literal kind ('sync_result' | 'async_pending' | 'degraded' | 'error') driving the discriminator. Verified by TestDispatchOutcomeRoundTrip::test_discriminator_distinguishes_variants_with_same_payload_shape and ::test_unknown_kind_is_rejected_by_discriminator.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `CapabilityResolution.retry_of: Optional[str] = None` added without breaking existing FEAT-FORGE-002 callers (verify by running existing tests in `tests/forge/discovery/test_discovery.py`).
completion_promises.evidence: Added 'retry_of: str | None = Field(default=None, ...)' to CapabilityResolution in src/forge/discovery/models.py as an append-only optional field. All 36 pre-existing tests in tests/forge/discovery/test_discovery.py continue to pass (re-run during this turn). New tests in TestCapabilityResolutionRetryOf confirm: default is None, the field carries a previous resolution_id when set, and the value round-trips through model_dump/model_validate.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Round-trip test: each variant of `DispatchOutcome` survives `model_dump()` -> `model_validate()` and the discriminator round-trips.
completion_promises.evidence: TestDispatchOutcomeRoundTrip contains one explicit round-trip test per variant (sync_result, async_pending, degraded, error) plus a discriminator-distinguishes test. Each test uses a TypeAdapter[DispatchOutcome] to validate the dumped dict goes through the discriminated union (the production consumption path) and asserts both isinstance(restored, ConcreteVariant) and restored == original. All four variant round-trips pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: The repository's pyproject.toml declares no [tool.ruff] / [tool.black] / [tool.flake8] section and the worktree contains no .ruff.toml / .pre-commit-config.yaml / .flake8 / setup.cfg files, so there is no project-configured lint/format tool to fail. Manually verified all new and modified files against the .claude/rules/code-style.md 88-char limit: every newly added line is <=88 characters. The single pre-existing 89-char line in src/forge/discovery/models.py:7 was not introduced by this task.
